_status: VERIFIED — created live in Kibana, actively matching real data (postgresql.backends=8)
_note: Threshold set to 5 (not the theoretical 80%-of-max=40) because 8 active connections is already above any reasonable idle baseline for this small assessment database — this rule is meant to prove the alerting pipeline works end-to-end, not model true production capacity.
name: Postgres: active connections above baseline

esqlQuery.esql: FROM metrics-* | WHERE data_stream.dataset LIKE "*postgresql*" AND postgresql.backends IS NOT NULL | WHERE postgresql.backends > 5

_status: VERIFIED — real data confirmed present via 'nginx_ingress_controller_ssl_expire_time_seconds' earlier in this build
name: NGINX: SSL cert expires within 14 days

_status: UNVERIFIED — field names corrected to match the 'system.*' Elastic Agent System-integration schema, but never tested against this deployment's live VM data. Confirm in Discover before relying on this.
name: VM: CPU > 85% for 5min

_status: UNVERIFIED — same caveat as above, field name not confirmed against your actual OTel-distro-shipped VM metrics (which may use hostmetrics receiver naming like 'system.filesystem.utilization' instead)
name: VM: Disk free < 10%
